label: Send Email (SMTP)
description: Send email via SMTP. Requires explicit per-node credentials — either literal values or {{secret:NAME}} workflow-secret references. No env-var fallback (2026-05-15 hardening: every flow that sends mail must carry its own credentials).

settings.name: smtpHost
settings.label: SMTP host
settings.description: SMTP server hostname. Literal value or {{secret:NAME}} reference to a workflow secret.

settings.name: smtpPort
settings.label: SMTP port
settings.description: SMTP port (default 587). Use 465 for implicit TLS. Accepts literal numeric strings or {{secret:SMTP_PORT}}.

settings.name: smtpUser
settings.label: SMTP username
settings.description: SMTP authentication username. Doubles as the `From:` address on the outbound message.

settings.name: smtpPasswordRef
settings.label: SMTP password
settings.description: SMTP password — reference a workflow secret via {{secret:NAME}}. Literal passwords accepted but strongly discouraged.

ai.shortDescription: Send email via SMTP. Credentials are REQUIRED on the node (no env fallback).
ai.whenToUse: When you need to send email. Configure SMTP via workflow secrets named SMTP_HOST / SMTP_PORT / SMTP_USER / SMTP_PASS (or any names you prefer) and reference them via {{secret:NAME}} on each field.
ai.examplePrompt: send a daily summary email to ops@example.com using the SendGrid relay
ai.promptHints: Use isHtml=false for plaintext bodies. Always reference passwords via {{secret:NAME}} — never put a literal password in the flow definition. Reference output via {{steps.<id>.result.sent}} and {{steps.<id>.result.messageId}}.
